questions.question: What is the inner product of a vector with itself?
options: A) zero
B) positive value
C) negative value
D) cannot be determined without more information
questions.explanation: The inner product of a vector with itself is the length (or norm) of the vector.

questions.question: Two vectors are said to be orthogonal if their inner product is zero. What is a property of orthogonal vectors?
options: A) They are equal
B) They have the same length
C) They are perpendicular
D) They are the same vector
questions.explanation: Orthogonal vectors are perpendicular to each other.

questions.question: Given an orthogonal set of vectors, what is the Gram-Schmidt process used to find?
options: A) An orthonormal set of vectors
B) A basis for the vector space
C) The least squares solution to a system of linear equations
D) The QR-factorization of a matrix
questions.explanation: The Gram-Schmidt process is used to find an orthonormal set of vectors from an orthogonal set of vectors.

questions.question: What is the purpose of QR-factorization in linear algebra?
options: A) To find the least squares solution to a system of linear equations
B) To find the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a matrix
C) To decompose a matrix into the product of an orthogonal matrix and an upper triangular matrix
D) To find the inverse of a matrix
questions.explanation: QR-factorization is used to decompose a matrix into the product of an orthogonal matrix and an upper triangular matrix.

questions.question: What is the least squares solution to a system of linear equations, and how is it related to the QR-factorization of the coefficient matrix A?
options: A) The solution is the vector that minimizes the sum of the squares of the residuals
B) The solution is the vector that maximizes the sum of the squares of the residuals
C) The solution is the vector that is orthogonal to the nullspace of A
D) The solution is the vector that is in the row space of A^T A
questions.explanation: The least squares solution is the vector that minimizes the sum of the squares of the residuals, and this solution can be found using the QR-factorization of the coefficient matrix A.
